items.title: Provider preset gallery empty on /portal/reports
items.original_feedback_summary: F6 (12:43 PM): 'En esta parte no deja ver las plantillas de los reportes.' Tester sees no Plantillas cards on /portal/reports.
items.suggested_fix: Reproduce on staging as jluna; capture the /api/v1/reports/_presets response. If 200 with items=[], inspect ReportActor.is_workspace_owner construction (apps/api/app/services/report_service.py:121) and the actor builder in apps/api/app/api/v1/reports.py — workspace owners with any non-empty roles tuple are excluded by the current property. Fix in seed data OR loosen the property. If non-200, lean on BL-008 to surface the failure.
acceptance_criteria: Logged in as jluna on staging, /portal/reports renders the three vendor_facing preset cards
The first preset is visually featured (existing F4 behavior preserved)
Unit test asserts presets_for_roles for jluna's actor shape returns three presets
test_plan: Backend: extend tests/test_reports_presets.py with jluna's seed shape
Frontend: integration test that mocks listPresets() success and asserts three cards

items.title: Add document anatomy + visual reference on /portal/upload
items.original_feedback_summary: F1 (10:56 AM) + F2 (11:12 AM): tester asks twice for per-document descriptions and a visual/image reference so non-technical providers know what to upload.
items.suggested_fix: Extend the requirement catalog with optional anatomy_es and sample_image fields. Surface both on the upload card and intake wizard. Ship sample images for the five highest-volume requirements first under apps/web/public/marketing/requirement-samples/.
acceptance_criteria: Five priority requirements show an anatomy paragraph in Spanish
Each of those five shows a sample image (or 'sample coming soon' note) with redaction confirmed
Layout holds at 1802x862 (tester viewport) plus the recently QA'd 360 / 768 / 1440 viewports
test_plan: Visual QA on five seeded requirements in both intake-wizard and standalone upload
Confirm Image lazy-loading does not regress LCP

items.title: Translate validation rule_code labels to plain Spanish
items.original_feedback_summary: F3 (11:28 AM): tester asks that 'file_exists / allowed_file_type' style labels be converted to human language.
items.suggested_fix: Add apps/web/lib/constants/validation.ts with RULE_CODE_LABELS_ES mapping every code defined in apps/api/app/services/prevalidation.py. Render the friendly label as primary in components/checkwise/validation-summary.tsx (line 29). Keep the raw rule_code in a small mono tag or title attribute for QA.
acceptance_criteria: No raw rule_code strings render on any user-facing surface
Every code emitted by prevalidation.py has a corresponding Spanish label
Tooltip / title still exposes the original code for QA reproducibility
test_plan: Unit test asserts the label map covers every value emitted by prevalidation.py (export a code-list fixture)
Visual regression on submissions detail page

items.title: Submitted-documents history + gauge clarity on /portal/dashboard
items.original_feedback_summary: F4 (12:14 PM): tester asks for a view-only list of uploaded documents grouped by Institución × Mes × Año, plus a clearer gauge that explains what is missing.
items.suggested_fix: Build a read-only ProviderHistoryList grouped by institution → year → month and place it under the existing 'Tu siguiente acción' strip. Extend the existing dashboard payload to include grouped submissions, or call the submissions list endpoint client-side. Add a 'Te faltan N documentos' subtitle to the compliance gauge with a chip listing the top missing requirement.
acceptance_criteria: History list groups submissions by Institución then Año then Mes
No edit/delete affordance on history rows (read-only)
Gauge subtitle shows missing count and at least one specific blocker when % < 100
Hero of the dashboard does not get pushed below the fold; history sits behind a 'Ver historial' expand if necessary
test_plan: Seed a workspace with 5 mixed submissions across 3 months and 4 institutions
Assert grouping order in the DOM

items.title: Eliminate stale RSC chunk errors after Vercel deploys
items.original_feedback_summary: RX-1 (console attachments under F1, F2, F3): repeated 'Failed to fetch RSC payload … Cannot read properties of undefined (reading call)' on portal/entra-a-tu-espacio, portal/upload, portal/onboarding, portal/dashboard.
items.user_role: all visitors with a tab spanning a deploy
items.suggested_fix: (a) Verify the new distDir override is inert on Vercel (Vercel cwd is ASCII so resolver returns .next). (b) Add a ChunkLoadError boundary at /portal/layout.tsx or /portal/error.tsx that triggers a soft reload on chunk failures. (c) Audit Vercel project settings for assetPrefix / outputFileTracingRoot. (d) Smoke a Vercel preview deploy.
acceptance_criteria: After a Vercel deploy, navigating between portal routes from a tab opened before the deploy does not produce the chunk error in the console
Any unavoidable hard reload happens silently (no flash, no error banner)
test_plan: Navigate to /portal/upload — confirm no console error

items.title: Calendar cell-level upload date + filename on /portal/calendar
items.original_feedback_summary: F7 (12:46 PM): tester asks the calendar surface day of upload + filename per cell, not only inside the drawer.
items.suggested_fix: Extend the CalendarItem schema (frontend + backend) with uploaded_at and filename for the latest submission of each slot. Render under the status badge on the desktop cell; mobile keeps the drawer pattern.
acceptance_criteria: Every populated cell shows the upload date (DD/MM/YYYY) and the filename truncated with ellipsis
Empty cells stay empty
Drawer still shows the full detail
test_plan: Seed workspace with at least one filled and one empty cell

items.title: Explain 'why am I not at 100%' on /portal/reports Compliance Pulse
items.original_feedback_summary: F5 (12:42 PM): tester praises stats clarity but asks for detail on what is keeping the expediente below 100%.
items.suggested_fix: Add a top-blocker chip to compliance-pulse-strip.tsx (e.g. '3 documentos pendientes en IMSS'). Source the chip text from the existing provider_current_state block executor.
acceptance_criteria: When compliance % < 100, the strip shows at least one specific blocker
When compliance % = 100, no chip renders
test_plan: Seed a 70% provider — assert the chip text matches the top blocker
Seed a 100% provider — assert no chip

items.title: Surface listPresets() failures in reports-list-view instead of silently empty
items.original_feedback_summary: F6 root-cause amplifier: the current silent catch in reports-list-view.tsx hides backend failures behind 'Tu rol todavía no tiene plantillas asignadas.'
items.suggested_fix: Add a presetsError state to reports-list-view.tsx. On non-401/403 errors, render an Alert above the preset gallery. Keep the existing role-empty message for legitimately empty preset sets.
acceptance_criteria: Mock listPresets() throwing a 500 renders a visible Alert
Mock listPresets() throwing a 403 renders the existing access-denied message
Mock listPresets() returning [] renders the existing 'tu rol todavía no tiene plantillas' empty state
test_plan: Unit test the three branches with mocked fetch

items.title: Collapse three vendor_facing presets into one configurable provider report
items.original_feedback_summary: F5 (12:42 PM, second half): tester suggests merging the three reports into one, with individual downloads configurable at export time.
items.suggested_fix: Behind a feature flag, define a new combined preset that includes the three current sections. Add per-section export in the report editor. Keep the legacy three presets for backward compatibility.
acceptance_criteria: One new preset card creates a report whose three sub-sections match the legacy three presets
Per-section export downloads only that section
Feature flag default OFF until validated
test_plan: Generate the combined report on staging; verify each section
Export one section; verify the file

raw_feedback.description: Considero que en esta parte se podrían poner las partes de cada documento, es decir, lo que debe de llevar un poco más a detalle y si es posible, una imagen o referencia visual sobre como es el documento, para guiar a quien va a cargarlo y que pueda tener idea de cual documento subir.

Esta parte está bien por que permite justificar mediante los comentarios el documento que se está cargando, la vista previa del documento puede servir para asegurarse de que el documento elegido es el correcto a cargar. De igual manera es buena idea si se pudieran tener las referencias de los documentos.

En esta parte, considero que lo está como file_exists o allowed_file_type y ese tipo de detalles, se conviertan el lenguaje humano, ya que, quien va a realizar la carga no podrá entenderlo del todo y probablemente tenga confusiones al momento de navegar en esta parte de la plataforma.

En este apartado puede que sea buena idea ver los documentos que se llevan cargados y que vayan ordenados ya sea por Institución, Mes y Año y que se puedan consultar, posiblemente editarlos pero se puede correr el riesgo de que estos puedan ser alterados o cambiados por otro documento que no sea respectivo de la institución, mes o año. Por ende, solo se sugiere que se visualicen el/los documento(s) que se han cargado mediante ese orden. Lo demás está bien, los estatus y avisos de la plataforma, aunque también es necesario ajustar la gráfica para que el cliente pueda entender mejor que le hace falta para completar su expediente.

En este apartado, la muestra de las estadísticas de los documentos es muy clara y logra el cometido del estatus del expediente, por ende, esto puede dar una retroalimentación mas justificada al cliente. Aunque también es buena idea si pudieran ver detalles sobre su expediente, en caso de que haga falta o motivos sobre el por que aún no se ha completado al 100% su progreso. En la parte de los reportes, considero que se pueden juntar los 3 en uno solo, y que si se quieren descargar individuales, estos se puedan configurar de mejor manera al momento de poderlos descargar.
En esta parte no deja ver las plantillas de los reportes.

Para este apartado, sugiero que se pueda visualizar de mejor manera el calendario, con detalle sobre el día en el que se hizo la carga y el nombre del documento que se cargó a la plataforma, para así tener una mejor pista y seguimiento de los documentos y el expediente en general. Para que cuando pase algo por X o Y situación, se pueda tomar como guia esta parte y aclarar un poco mejor las situaciones que puedan acontecer.

Failed to fetch RSC payload — TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'call') at webpack-9513878bb031f797.js. Repeated across four portal routes within a single session, indicating stale webpack chunk references after a Vercel deploy.
